ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON MAR-11-2008 IN 206, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE PURSUANT TO CCP 36(D) AND TO ADVANCE CURRENT TRIAL DATE IS GRANTED. THE TRIAL DATE OF 9/15/08 IS ADVANCED TO 6/16/08 IN DEPT. 206 AT 9:30 AM. THE CURRENT SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WILL BE VACATED, AND PLAINTIFF TO GIVE NOTICE OF THE NEW SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. OUTSTANDING AUTHORIZATION IS DUE BY 3/17/08. FIRST LOOK IS SHORTENED TO 5 DAYS. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE ALL PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND CAUSATION WITNESSES AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 4/25/08, AND SHALL ALSO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS THEY INTEND TO USE AT TRIAL AS TO PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY THAT DATE. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. DEFENDANTS TO PROVIDE INFORMATION REGARDING PROPOSITION 51 WITNESSES AND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES BY 5/12/08. IF DEFENDANTS LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LATER THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO THREE EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: KATHERINE FEINSTEIN; CLERK: D.LOK; REPORTER: SUSAN LEE, CSR #4280

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CHRYSLER, L.L.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. THERE IS EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF WORKED ON ORIGINAL CHRYSLER BRAKES IN BOTH DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. THIS EVIDENCE HAS NOT BEEN NEGATED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORY RESPONSES AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION (FALSE REPRESENTATION), FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION (INTENTIONAL TORT) AND EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION (STRICT LIABILITY RELATED TO CLUTCHES AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES). PLAINTIF FAILS TO PRESENT EVIDENCE ON THESE CAUSES OF ACTION THAT RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THIRD, FOURTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO FIRST, SECOND, FIFTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON EXPOSURE TO NEW BRAKES. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-05-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CHRYSLER, L.L.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: STEPHEN T. BLACKBURN, ESQUIRE (BARON & BUDD, 310.860.0476 & 800.887.6989) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. LAUREN M. MICHALS, ESQUIRE (THELEN REID, 415.369.7029) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. THERE IS EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF WORKED ON ORIGINAL CHRYSLER BRAKES IN BOTH DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. THIS EVIDENCE HAS NOT BEEN NEGATED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORY RESPONSES AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION (FALSE REPRESENTATION), FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION (INTENTIONAL TORT) AND EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION (STRICT LIABILITY RELATED TO CLUTCHES AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES). PLAINTIF FAILS TO PRESENT EVIDENCE ON THESE CAUSES OF ACTION THAT RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THIRD, FOURTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO FIRST, SECOND, FIFTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON EXPOSURE TO NEW BRAKES. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
